articles.title: Idaho Health Committee Advances Vaccine Exemption Bill
articles.summary: The Idaho House Health & Welfare committee voted to advance a bill expanding religious and philosophical exemptions for childhood vaccines. Chair Megan Blanksma allowed the bill to proceed despite opposition from medical groups.

articles.title: Rep. Carter Pushes Pharmacy Access Act Through Energy & Commerce
articles.summary: Rep. Buddy Carter (R-GA), the only pharmacist in Congress, successfully moved his Pharmacy Access Act through the Energy and Commerce committee. The bill would expand pharmacist authority to administer vaccines.

articles.title: Pediatrician-Turned-Lawmaker Champions Vaccine Research Funding
articles.summary: Rep. Yadira Caraveo (D-CO), a pediatrician, introduced legislation to increase NIH funding for vaccine safety research. The bill has drawn bipartisan interest from members with medical backgrounds.

articles.title: Senate HELP Committee to Hold Hearings on Vaccine Safety Transparency
articles.summary: The Senate HELP committee announced upcoming hearings on vaccine safety data transparency. New member Dave McCormick (R-PA) signaled interest in ensuring public access to safety monitoring data.

articles.title: Texas Lawmakers Debate Medical Freedom Act
articles.summary: Texas state legislators debated a Medical Freedom Act that would prohibit employer vaccine mandates. Sen. Cornyn expressed cautious support for 'balanced' approach to medical decisions.

articles.title: Anti-Vaccine Bills Surge in State Legislatures Across the Country
articles.summary: A new wave of anti-vaccine legislation has been introduced in over 30 state legislatures, with bills targeting school immunization requirements and employer mandates. Public health officials express concern about rising exemption rates.
